To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $I$ is the identity matrix and $\lambda$ is the variable.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{array}\right) $$

To simplify the calculation, let's first find a common denominator for all entries, which is 16.
$$ A = \frac{1}{16} \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right) $$
Let $B = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right)$, so $A = \frac{1}{16}B$.

The characteristic polynomial is $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
$$ p(\lambda) = \det\left( \frac{1}{16}B - \lambda I \right) = \det\left( \frac{1}{16}(B - 16\lambda I) \right) $$
Since $A$ is a $3 \times 3$ matrix, factoring out $\frac{1}{16}$ from each of the 3 rows gives:
$$ p(\lambda) = \left(\frac{1}{16}\right)^3 \det(B - 16\lambda I) $$
